summary: Maintainer-filed enhancement to add scroll wheel support to SkiaSharp's Tizen MAUI views by handling WheelEvent in the Tizen SKTouchHandler with v120 normalization (120 units = one discrete mouse notch), as part of the cross-platform wheel standardization project (#3533).

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Tizen NUI platform, SkiaSharp MAUI views, Tizen.NUI.Wheel API

analysis.summary: Well-specified implementation task from the maintainer. The Tizen SKTouchHandler currently only handles TouchEvent (Down/Up/Motion/Leave/Interrupted). It needs a WheelEvent handler that normalizes Tizen's Z values (±1 per tick, positive=down) to v120 convention (positive=up, ×120 scale). The SKTouchAction.WheelChanged enum value and SKTouchEventArgs.WheelDelta property already exist; only the Tizen platform handler needs the new event wiring.
analysis.rationale: Classified as enhancement because it adds new capability (wheel event support) to an existing view component. Area is SkiaSharp.Views.Maui since the change is in the Tizen platform handler under source/SkiaSharp.Views.Maui/. The Windows handler already implements the pattern, serving as a reference. This is one of seven sibling issues under the v120 parent (#3533).

keySignals.interpretation: Part of systematic cross-platform wheel standardization project.

keySignals.text: Reference: Windows handler in Platform/Windows/SKTouchHandler.cs for the pattern
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer explicitly points to the implementation pattern to follow.

keySignals.text: Must negate — opposite of v120 convention (where positive = up)
keySignals.interpretation: Tizen Z sign convention is inverted vs v120; normalization formula is -Z * 120.

codeInvestigation.finding: SetEnabled only subscribes to view.TouchEvent. No WheelEvent subscription exists. The handler has no wheel-related code at all.
Windows handler subscribes to PointerWheelChanged alongside other pointer events, and fires SKTouchAction.WheelChanged with MouseWheelDelta — this is the reference implementation pattern.
WheelDelta property (int) and SKTouchAction.WheelChanged enum value already exist in the shared event args — no changes needed to the shared API surface.

resolution.hypothesis: The Tizen SKTouchHandler needs a WheelEvent handler that normalizes Z values to v120 and fires SKTouchAction.WheelChanged.

proposals.title: Add WheelEvent handler to Tizen SKTouchHandler
proposals.description: Subscribe to view.WheelEvent in SetEnabled, add OnWheelEvent handler that computes wheelDelta = -wheel.Z * 120, and fires SKTouchAction.WheelChanged with SKTouchDeviceType.Mouse and inContact=false. Follow the Windows handler pattern.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add WheelEvent handler to Tizen SKTouchHandler
resolution.recommendedReason: The issue provides complete implementation specification including normalization formula, sign convention, and reference handler. Small, well-scoped change.

actionability.reason: Well-specified implementation task filed by the maintainer as part of the v120 standardization project. Ready for implementation.

actions.description: Correct area label from area/SkiaSharp to area/SkiaSharp.Views.Maui and add platform/partner labels
Link to parent v120 standardization issue
